headline: Finalization pausable >7 days by GateSeal; claims of finalized exits always unblocked
rationale: E1: Request-placement: requestWithdrawals, requestWithdrawalsWithPermit, requestWithdrawalsWstETH, requestWithdrawalsWstETHWithPermit. Claim: claimWithdrawals, claimWithdrawalsTo, claimWithdrawal. E2: Request functions guarded by _checkResumed(); claim functions carry no pause guard—explicitly documented 'Claiming finalized requests will still be available.' E3/E4: Emergency path is GateSeal multisig (one-time-use, auto-expires), seal duration 11 days; governance (Lido DAO Aragon vote, 3-day Dual-Governance timelock) holds PAUSE_ROLE and can call pauseFor(PAUSE_INFINITELY) indefinitely via vote. E5: Queue duration days-to-weeks, no hard cap or daily limit documented. E6: No permissionless escape hatch. E7: All functions callable via Etherscan write tab. Steel-man red: PAUSE_INFINITELY is callable; governance can pause finalization indefinitely, trapping unfinalized requests. Steel-man orange: GateSeal capped at 11 days (>7d); indefinite pause requires DAO vote not unilateral action; finalized claims are never blocked. Steel-man green: claims of finalized exits are always unblocked; GateSeal auto-expires and is one-time; Dual Governance gives stETH holders veto. Choosing orange because GateSeal pause (11 days) exceeds the 7-day green threshold, and governance can reach indefinite finalization pause, blocking new exit requests from reaching claimable state.

evidence.shows: Deployed WithdrawalQueue ERC721 proxy contract on Ethereum mainnet
WithdrawalQueueERC721 implementation contract; source confirms claimWithdrawals has no pause guard, pauseFor/pauseUntil gated by PAUSE_ROLE
Docs confirm pauseUntil/pauseFor only block request placement and finalization; claimWithdrawals/claimWithdrawalsTo have no pause guard
Source: PAUSE_ROLE, pauseFor(PAUSE_INFINITELY), pauseUntil; claimWithdrawalsTo has no _checkResumed modifier
GateSeal max seal duration hardcoded to 14 days; one-time use; operated by a sealing committee multisig
Current GateSeal duration is 11 days (2x governance reaction time + 1 day); after use, DAO can vote to extend pause
GateSeal Committee multisig authorized to trigger GateSeal contracts; pause right is single-use and auto-expires
WithdrawalQueue ERC721 proxy at 0x889edC2eDab5f40e902b864aD4d7AdE8E412F9B1; protocol version v3.0.1
Lido DAO Aragon voting with Dual Governance 3-day minimum timelock; DAO can execute protocol changes including pause calls

unknowns: E3: Could not perform a live Etherscan read call to getRoleMemberCount/getRoleMember on WithdrawalQueue 0x889edc2 to enumerate current on-chain PAUSE_ROLE holders and confirm the exact GateSeal address currently assigned; docs confirm GateSeal committee holds it but live slot value unverified in this run.
E5: No documented hard maximum queue duration or per-day withdrawal cap found in sources; duration is variable depending on validator exit queue depth and oracle reporting cadence.
E6: No permissionless escape-hatch or forced-exit mechanism for adversarial-admin scenarios was found in any examined source.
